title: Xylanthemum tianschanicum
intro: Xylanthemum tianschanicum, also known as Xylanthemum, is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of China, belonging to the daisy family (Compositae). The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-modulating properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Xylanthemum tianschanicum contains bioactive constituents such as triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as dried and processed into capsules, tablets, and granules for various medicinal applications.
taxonomy: Xylanthemum tianschanicum is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the tribe Anthemideae and is native to Central Asia, specifically in the mountainous regions of China, Kyrgyzstan, Kazakhstan, and Tajikistan. The plant is classified as an annual herb, typically growing up to 30 centimeters in height. Its classification is closely related to other plants in the same family, such as the dandelion and the sunflower. By understanding its classification, scientists can better comprehend the plant's evolutionary history and its relationships with other organisms.
